title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for score reconciliation, waves, safe spawns, pacing, and difficulty options and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for fair progression and difficulty model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, c…
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Score Ledger · changed case · Quiz Score Ledger
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 6309 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Difficulty Access: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 5830 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Access.
steps.why: Retrieving Difficulty Access through the concrete fair progression and difficulty model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Access, keep Difficulty Access connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.
